title: Primula balearica
taxonomy: Primula balearica is a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. This plant is native to the Balearic Islands, specifically in the Mediterranean region. It belongs to the genus Primula, which consists of over 400 species of primroses. The classification of Primula balearica places it within the order Ericales and the clade asterids. Further classification groups it under the clade euasterids I and the clade asterids.

intro: Primula balearica, also known as Balearic primrose, is a perennial plant species native to the Balearic Islands in the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Primula balearica include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Primula balearica is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to support immune function.
